El novedoso método de generación de datos impulsado por la percepción social garantiza la relevancia en el mundo real y la verificabilidad, al tiempo que permite actualizaciones continuas con consultas nuevas.

title: SUN: Shared Use of Next-token Prediction for Efficient Multi-LLM Disaggregated Serving
summary: New approach for multi-LLM serving that shares decode execution across models, improving GPU utilization and achieving 2x throughput improvement while maintaining accuracy comparable to full fine-tuning.
content:
arXiv:2603.02599v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In multi-model LLM serving, decode execution remains inefficient due to model-specific resource partitioning: since cross-model batching is not possible, memory-bound decoding often suffers from severe GPU underutilization, especially under skewed workloads. We propose Shared Use of Next-token Prediction (SUN), the first approach that enables cross-model sharing of decode execution in disaggregated multi-LLM serving. SUN decomposes a decoder-only Transformer into a prefill module and a decode module, and fine-tunes only the task-specific prefill module, enabling a frozen decode module to be shared across models. This design enables a model-agnostic decode routing policy that balances decode requests across shared workers to maximize utilization. Across diverse tasks and model families, SUN achieves accuracy comparable to full fine-tuning while maintaining system throughput with fewer decode workers. In particular, SUN improves throughput per GPU by up to 2.0x over conventional disaggregation while keeping time-per-output-token (TPOT) within 5%. SUN inherently enables and facilitates low-bit decoding; with Quantized SUN (QSUN), it achieves a 45% speedup with comparable accuracy to SUN while preserving the benefits of shared decoding.

zh.title: SUN：用于高效多LLM分解服务的下一个令牌预测的共享使用
zh.summary: 一种多LLM服务的新方法，在模型之间共享解码执行，改进GPU利用率，实现2倍吞吐量改进，同时保持与完全微调相当的准确性。

fr.title: SUN: Utilisation partagée de la prédiction du prochain jeton pour un service multi-LLM efficace et désagrégé
fr.summary: Nouvelle approche pour la fourniture de services multi-LLM qui partage l'exécution du décodage entre les modèles, améliore l'utilisation du GPU et réalise une amélioration du débit 2x tout en maintenant une précision comparable au fine-tuning complet.

de.title: SUN: Gemeinsame Nutzung der Vorhersage des nächsten Tokens für effizientes disaggregiertes Multi-LLM-Serving
de.summary: Neuer Ansatz für Multi-LLM-Serving, der die Dekodierungsausführung zwischen Modellen teilt, die GPU-Auslastung verbessert und einen 2x-Durchsatzverbesserung erzielt und dabei eine mit vollständigem Fine-Tuning vergleichbare Genauigkeit beibehält.

es.title: SUN: Uso compartido de la predicción del siguiente token para un servicio multi-LLM eficiente desagregado
es.summary: Nuevo enfoque para el servicio multi-LLM que comparte la ejecución de decodificación entre modelos, mejora la utilización de GPU y logra una mejora de rendimiento 2x mientras mantiene una precisión comparable al fine-tuning completo.

title: AgentAssay: Token-Efficient Regression Testing for Non-Deterministic AI Agent Workflows
summary: Framework for cost-efficient regression testing of AI agents using statistical methods and behavioral fingerprinting, achieving 78-100% cost reduction while maintaining rigorous verification guarantees across diverse models.
content:
arXiv:2603.02601v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Autonomous AI agents are deployed at unprecedented scale, yet no principled methodology exists for
  verifying that an agent has not regressed after changes to its prompts, tools, models, or
  orchestration logic. We present AgentAssay, the first token-efficient framework for regression
  testing non-deterministic AI agent workflows, achieving 78-100% cost reduction while maintaining
  rigorous statistical guarantees. Our contributions include: (1) stochastic three-valued verdicts
  (PASS/FAIL/INCONCLUSIVE) grounded in hypothesis testing; (2) five-dimensional agent coverage metrics;
  (3) agent-specific mutation testing operators; (4) metamorphic relations for agent workflows; (5)
  CI/CD deployment gates as statistical decision procedures; (6) behavioral fingerprinting that maps
  execution traces to compact vectors, enabling multivariate regression detection; (7) adaptive budget
  optimization calibrating trial counts to behavioral variance; and (8) trace-first offline analysis
  enabling zero-cost testing on production traces. Experiments across 5 models (GPT-5.2, Claude Sonnet
  4.6, Mistral-Large-3, Llama-4-Maverick, Phi-4), 3 scenarios, and 7,605 trials demonstrate that
  behavioral fingerprinting achieves 86% detection power where binary testing has 0%, SPRT reduces
  trials by 78%, and the full pipeline achieves 100% cost savings through trace-first analysis.
  Implementation: 20,000+ lines of Python, 751 tests, 10 framework adapters.

zh.title: AgentAssay：非确定性AI代理工作流的令牌高效回归测试
zh.summary: 用于AI代理成本高效回归测试的框架，使用统计方法和行为指纹识别，在跨越不同模型的情况下实现78-100%的成本削减，同时保持严格的验证保证。

fr.title: AgentAssay: Tests de régression efficaces en jetons pour les flux de travail non déterministes d'agents d'IA
fr.summary: Cadre pour les tests de régression économes en jetons des agents d'IA utilisant des méthodes statistiques et l'empreinte comportementale, réalisant une réduction des coûts de 78-100% tout en maintenant des garanties de vérification rigoureuses dans l'ensemble des modèles divers.

de.title: AgentAssay: Token-effiziente Regressionstests für nicht-deterministische KI-Agent-Workflows
de.summary: Rahmenwerk für kostengünstige Regressionstests von KI-Agenten unter Verwendung statistischer Methoden und Verhaltens-Fingerprinting, wobei eine Kostenreduktion von 78-100% erreicht wird, während gleichzeitig rigorose Verifikationsgarantien über verschiedene Modelle hinweg gewährleistet werden.

es.title: AgentAssay: Pruebas de regresión eficientes en tokens para flujos de trabajo de agentes de IA no deterministas
es.summary: Marco para pruebas de regresión rentables de agentes de IA utilizando métodos estadísticos e identificación de comportamiento, logrando una reducción de costos del 78-100% mientras se mantienen garantías de verificación rigurosas en modelos diversos.

title: See and Remember: A Multimodal Agent for Web Traversal
summary: Multimodal agent architecture combining visual grounding and explicit memory systems to prevent navigation loops and enable structured backtracking, improving web traversal performance by 28.7%.
content:
arXiv:2603.02626v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Autonomous web navigation requires agents to perceive complex visual environments and maintain long-term context, yet current Large Language Model (LLM) based agents often struggle with spatial disorientation and navigation loops. In this paper, we propose generally applicable V-GEMS(Visual Grounding and Explicit Memory System), a robust multimodal agent architecture designed for precise and resilient web traversal. Our agent integrates visual grounding to resolve ambiguous interactive elements and introduces an explicit memory stack with state tracking. This dual mechanism allows the agent to maintain a structured map of its traversal path, enabling valid backtracking and preventing cyclical failures in deep navigation tasks. We also introduce an updatable dynamic benchmark to rigorously evaluate adaptability. Experiments show V-GEMS significantly dominates the WebWalker baseline, achieving a substantial 28.7% performance gain. Code is available at https://github.com/Vaultttttttttttt/V-GEMS.

zh.title: 看和记住：用于网络遍历的多模态代理
zh.summary: 多模态代理架构，结合视觉基础和显式记忆系统，以防止导航循环并启用结构化回溯，改进网络遍历性能28.7%。

fr.title: Voir et Mémoriser: Un agent multimodal pour la traversée du Web
fr.summary: Architecture d'agent multimodal combinant la mise à la terre visuelle et les systèmes de mémoire explicite pour prévenir les boucles de navigation et permettre le retour arrière structuré, améliorant les performances de traversée du Web de 28,7%.

de.title: Sehen und Merken: Ein multimodaler Agent für Web-Traversierung
de.summary: Multimodale Agent-Architektur, die visuelle Grundlagen und explizite Speichersysteme kombiniert, um Navigationsschleifen zu verhindern und strukturiertes Backtracking zu ermöglichen, wodurch die Web-Traversierungsleistung um 28,7% verbessert wird.

es.title: Ver y Recordar: Un agente multimodal para el recorrido web
es.summary: Arquitectura de agente multimodal que combina anclaje visual y sistemas de memoria explícita para prevenir bucles de navegación y permitir retroceso estructurado, mejorando el rendimiento de recorrido web en un 28,7%.

title: SorryDB: Can AI Provers Complete Real-World Lean Theorems?
summary: Dynamic benchmark of 1000+ real-world Lean theorem proving tasks from GitHub projects, demonstrating that complementary approaches combining LLMs, agentic methods, and symbolic provers achieve better results than any single approach.
content:
arXiv:2603.02668v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present SorryDB, a dynamically-updating benchmark of open Lean tasks drawn from 78 real world formalization projects on GitHub. Unlike existing static benchmarks, often composed of competition problems, hillclimbing the SorryDB benchmark will yield tools that are aligned to the community needs, more usable by mathematicians, and more capable of understanding complex dependencies. Moreover, by providing a continuously updated stream of tasks, SorryDB mitigates test-set contamination and offers a robust metric for an agent's ability to contribute to novel formal mathematics projects. We evaluate a collection of approaches, including generalist large language models, agentic approaches, and specialized symbolic provers, over a selected snapshot of 1000 tasks from SorryDB. We show that current approaches are complementary: even though an agentic approach based on Gemini Flash is the most performant, it is not strictly better than other off-the-shelf large-language models, specialized provers, or even a curated list of Lean tactics.

zh.title: SorryDB：AI证明器能完成真实的Lean定理吗？
zh.summary: 来自GitHub项目的1000多个真实Lean定理证明任务的动态基准，演示结合LLM、代理方法和符号证明器的互补方法比任何单一方法都能取得更好的结果。

fr.title: SorryDB : Les prouveurs IA peuvent-ils compléter les théorèmes Lean du monde réel ?
fr.summary: Un benchmark dynamique de plus de 1000 tâches réelles de preuve de théorème Lean provenant de projets GitHub, démontrant que les approches complémentaires combinant des LLM, des méthodes agentiques et des prouveurs symboliques obtiennent de meilleurs résultats que n'importe quelle approche unique.

de.title: SorryDB: Können KI-Prover reale Lean-Theoreme vervollständigen?
de.summary: Dynamischer Benchmark von über 1000 realen Lean-Theorembeweisaufgaben aus GitHub-Projekten, der demonstriert, dass komplementäre Ansätze, die LLMs, agentische Methoden und symbolische Prover kombinieren, bessere Ergebnisse erzielen als jeder einzelne Ansatz.

es.title: SorryDB: ¿Pueden los probadores de IA completar teoremas Lean del mundo real?
es.summary: Benchmark dinámico de más de 1000 tareas reales de prueba de teoremas Lean de proyectos de GitHub, demostrando que los enfoques complementarios que combinan LLM, métodos de agentes e demostradores simbólicos logran mejores resultados que cualquier enfoque único.

title: LLMs for High-Frequency Decision-Making: Normalized Action Reward-Guided Consistency Policy Optimization
summary: Novel optimization method for LLMs in high-frequency decision-making through normalized action rewards and consistency loss, delivering superior performance on UAV pursuit tasks with strong generalization to unseen scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2603.02680v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) form the cornerstone of sequential decision-making agent development, they have inherent limitations in high-frequency decision tasks. Existing research mainly focuses on discrete embodied decision scenarios with low-frequency and significant semantic differences in state space (e.g., household planning). These methods suffer from limited performance in high-frequency decision-making tasks, since high-precision numerical state information in such tasks undergoes frequent updates with minimal fluctuations, and exhibiting policy misalignment between the learned sub-tasks and composite tasks. To address these issues, this paper proposes Normalized Action Reward guided Consistency Policy Optimization (NAR-CP). 1) Our method first acquires predefined dense rewards from environmental feedback of candidate actions via reward functions, then completes reward shaping through normalization, and theoretically verifies action reward normalization does not impair optimal policy. 2) To reduce policy misalignment in composite tasks, we use LLMs to infer sub-observation candidate actions and generate joint policies, with consistency loss ensuring precise alignment between global semantic policies and sub-semantic policies. Experiments on UAV pursuit, a typical high-frequency task, show our method delivers superior performance on independent and composite tasks with excellent generalization to unseen tasks.

zh.title: 用于高频决策的LLM：标准化动作奖励引导的一致性策略优化
zh.summary: 通过标准化动作奖励和一致性损失为LLM高频决策提供的新型优化方法，在无人机追踪任务上提供卓越的性能，并对未见过的场景具有强大的泛化能力。

fr.title: LLM pour la prise de décision haute fréquence : optimisation de la politique de cohérence guidée par la récompense d'action normalisée
fr.summary: Une nouvelle méthode d'optimisation pour les LLM dans la prise de décision haute fréquence grâce aux récompenses d'action normalisées et à la perte de cohérence, offrant des performances supérieures dans les tâches de poursuite de drone avec une forte généralisation à des scénarios non vus.

de.title: LLM für Entscheidungsfindung mit hoher Frequenz: Optimierung der Konsistenzrichtlinie durch normalisierte Aktionsbelohnungen
de.summary: Eine neue Optimierungsmethode für LLMs bei hochfrequenter Entscheidungsfindung durch normalisierte Aktionsbelohnungen und Konsistenz-Verlust, die überlegene Leistung bei UAV-Verfolgungsaufgaben mit starker Generalisierung auf ungesehene Szenarien bietet.

es.title: LLM para toma de decisiones de alta frecuencia: optimización de política de coherencia guiada por recompensa de acción normalizada
es.summary: Un nuevo método de optimización para LLM en toma de decisiones de alta frecuencia a través de recompensas de acciones normalizadas y pérdida de coherencia, proporcionando un rendimiento superior en tareas de persecución de UAV con fuerte generalización a escenarios no vistos.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Robots via Retrieve-Reason-Act
summary: Framework enabling robots to actively retrieve visual procedural documentation and ground it to 3D environments for task planning, significantly outperforming zero-shot reasoning on long-horizon assembly tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.02688v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: To achieve general-purpose utility, we argue that robots must evolve from passive executors into active Information Retrieval users. In strictly zero-shot settings where no prior demonstrations exist, robots face a critical information gap, such as the exact sequence required to assemble a complex furniture kit, that cannot be satisfied by internal parametric knowledge (common sense) or past internal memory. While recent robotic works attempt to use search before action, they primarily focus on retrieving past kinematic trajectories (analogous to searching internal memory) or text-based safety rules (searching for constraints). These approaches fail to address the core information need of active task construction: acquiring unseen procedural knowledge from external, unstructured documentation. In this paper, we define the paradigm as Retrieval-Augmented Robotics (RAR), empowering the robot with the information-seeking capability that bridges the gap between visual documentation and physical actuation. We formulate the task execution as an iterative Retrieve-Reason-Act loop: the robot or embodied agent actively retrieves relevant visual procedural manuals from an unstructured corpus, grounds the abstract 2D diagrams to 3D physical parts via cross-modal alignment, and synthesizes executable plans. We validate this paradigm on a challenging long-horizon assembly benchmark. Our experiments demonstrate that grounding robotic planning in retrieved visual documents significantly outperforms baselines relying on zero-shot reasoning or few-shot example retrieval. This work establishes the basis of RAR, extending the scope of Information Retrieval from answering user queries to driving embodied physical actions.

zh.title: 通过检索-推理-行动的检索增强型机器人
zh.summary: 一个框架，使机器人能够主动检索视觉程序文档并将其接地到3D环境中以进行任务规划，在长期装配任务中明显优于零样本推理。

fr.title: Robots augmentés par récupération via Récupérer-Raisonner-Agir
fr.summary: Un framework permettant aux robots de récupérer activement la documentation procédurale visuelle et de l'ancrer dans des environnements 3D pour la planification des tâches, surpassant significativement le raisonnement sans exemple sur les tâches d'assemblage à long horizon.

de.title: Abruf-erweiterte Roboter über Abrufen-Begründen-Handeln
de.summary: Ein Framework, das Robotern ermöglicht, visuell Verfahrensdokumentation aktiv abzurufen und sie in 3D-Umgebungen für die Aufgabenplanung zu verankern, wobei es das aufgabenlose Denken bei langfristigen Montageaufgaben erheblich übertrifft.

es.title: Robots aumentados por recuperación mediante Recuperar-Razonar-Actuar
es.summary: Un framework que permite a los robots recuperar activamente documentación de procedimientos visuales y vincularla a entornos 3D para la planificación de tareas, superando significativamente el razonamiento sin ejemplos en tareas de ensamblaje de largo horizonte.

title: FinTexTS: Financial Text-Paired Time-Series Dataset via Semantic-Based and Multi-Level Pairing
summary: Large-scale financial dataset pairing stock prices with semantically-matched news at multiple contextual levels using LLMs, improving forecasting performance through sophisticated text-time-series alignment.
content:
arXiv:2603.02702v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The financial domain involves a variety of important time-series problems. Recently, time-series analysis methods that jointly leverage textual and numerical information have gained increasing attention. Accordingly, numerous efforts have been made to construct text-paired time-series datasets in the financial domain. However, financial markets are characterized by complex interdependencies, in which a company's stock price is influenced not only by company-specific events but also by events in other companies and broader macroeconomic factors. Existing approaches that pair text with financial time-series data based on simple keyword matching often fail to capture such complex relationships. To address this limitation, we propose a semantic-based and multi-level pairing framework. Specifically, we extract company-specific context for the target company from SEC filings and apply an embedding-based matching mechanism to retrieve semantically relevant news articles based on this context. Furthermore, we classify news articles into four levels (macro-level, sector-level, related company-level, and target-company level) using large language models (LLMs), enabling multi-level pairing of news articles with the target company. Applying this framework to publicly-available news datasets, we construct \textbf{FinTexTS}, a new large-scale text-paired stock price dataset. Experimental results on \textbf{FinTexTS} demonstrate the effectiveness of our semantic-based and multi-level pairing strategy in stock price forecasting. In addition to publicly-available news underlying \textbf{FinTexTS}, we show that applying our method to proprietary yet carefully curated news sources leads to higher-quality paired data and improved stock price forecasting performance.

zh.title: FinTexTS：金融文本配对时间序列数据集（通过语义基础和多层次配对）
zh.summary: 大规模金融数据集，使用大型语言模型在多个上下文级别将股票价格与语义匹配的新闻配对，通过复杂的文本-时间序列对齐改进预测性能。

fr.title: FinTexTS : Ensemble de données de séries chronologiques appariées avec texte financier via appairage sémantique et multi-niveaux
fr.summary: Grand ensemble de données financières appairant les prix des actions avec des nouvelles sémantiquement appariées à plusieurs niveaux contextuels à l'aide de modèles de langage, améliorant les performances de prévision grâce à un alignement texte-série chronologique sophistiqué.

de.title: FinTexTS: Finanztextgepaarte Zeitreihendatensatz durch semantische und mehrstufige Paarung
de.summary: Großer Finanzdatensatz, der Aktienkurse mit semantisch abgestimmten Nachrichten auf mehreren Kontextebenen unter Verwendung von LLMs paart und die Prognoseleistung durch ausgefeilte Text-Zeitreihen-Ausrichtung verbessert.

es.title: FinTexTS: Conjunto de datos de series de tiempo emparejadas con texto financiero mediante emparejamiento semántico y multinivel
es.summary: Conjunto de datos financiero a gran escala que empareja precios de acciones con noticias semánticamente coincidentes en múltiples niveles contextuales usando LLMs, mejorando el rendimiento de pronóstico a través de un alineamiento sofisticado de texto-series de tiempo.

title: A Natural Language Agentic Approach to Study Affective Polarization
summary: Multi-agent framework simulating virtual communities to study affective polarization on social media, offering flexible computational approach to analyzing complex social dynamics traditionally studied through human subjects.
content:
arXiv:2603.02711v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Affective polarization has been central to political and social studies, with growing focus on social media, where partisan divisions are often exacerbated. Real-world studies tend to have limited scope, while simulated studies suffer from insufficient high-quality training data, as manually labeling posts is labor-intensive and prone to subjective biases. The lack of adequate tools to formalize different definitions of affective polarization across studies complicates result comparison and hinders interoperable frameworks. We present a multi-agent model providing a comprehensive approach to studying affective polarization in social media. To operationalize our framework, we develop a platform leveraging large language models (LLMs) to construct virtual communities where agents engage in discussions. We showcase the potential of our platform by (1) analyzing questions related to affective polarization, as explored in social science literature, providing a fresh perspective on this phenomenon, and (2) introducing scenarios that allow observation and measurement of polarization at different levels of granularity and abstraction. Experiments show that our platform is a flexible tool for computational studies of complex social dynamics such as affective polarization. It leverages advanced agent models to simulate rich, context-sensitive interactions and systematically explore research questions traditionally addressed through human-subject studies.

zh.title: 研究情感极化的自然语言代理方法
zh.summary: 多代理框架模拟虚拟社区研究社交媒体的情感极化，提供灵活的计算方法来分析传统上通过人类受试者研究的复杂社会动力学。

fr.title: Une approche agentic du langage naturel pour étudier la polarisation affective
fr.summary: Cadre multi-agents simulant des communautés virtuelles pour étudier la polarisation affective sur les réseaux sociaux, offrant une approche computationnelle flexible pour analyser les dynamiques sociales complexes traditionnellement étudiées par des sujets humains.

de.title: Ein natürlichsprachlicher agentenbasierter Ansatz zur Untersuchung affektiver Polarisierung
de.summary: Multi-Agent-Framework, das virtuelle Gemeinschaften simuliert, um affektive Polarisierung in sozialen Medien zu untersuchen, und bietet einen flexiblen rechnerischen Ansatz zur Analyse komplexer sozialer Dynamiken, die traditionell durch menschliche Versuchspersonen untersucht werden.

es.title: Un enfoque de lenguaje natural agéntico para estudiar la polarización afectiva
es.summary: Marco multi-agente que simula comunidades virtuales para estudiar la polarización afectiva en redes sociales, ofreciendo un enfoque computacional flexible para analizar dinámicas sociales complejas tradicionalmente estudiadas a través de sujetos humanos.

title: EvoSkill: Automated Skill Discovery for Multi-Agent Systems
summary: Self-evolving framework automatically discovering reusable agent skills through failure analysis and structured refinement, achieving 7.3-12.1% accuracy improvements with demonstrated zero-shot transferability across tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.02766v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Coding agents are increasingly used as general-purpose problem solvers, but their flexibility does not by itself confer the domain expertise needed for specialized tasks. Recent work addresses this through \textit{agent skills}: reusable workflows, and code, that augment agents with domain-specific capabilities. Most skills today are hand-crafted, and existing evolutionary approaches optimize low-level artifacts (e.g. prompts \& code) that are tightly coupled to specific models and tasks. We introduce \textbf{EvoSkill}, a self-evolving framework that automatically discovers and refines agent skills through iterative failure analysis. EvoSkill analyzes execution failures, proposes new skills or edits to existing ones, and materializes them into structured, reusable skill folders. A Pareto frontier of agent programs governs selection, retaining only skills that improve held-out validation performance while the underlying model remains frozen. We evaluate EvoSkill on two benchmarks: OfficeQA, a grounded reasoning benchmark over U.S.\ Treasury data, where it improves exact-match accuracy by \textbf{7.3\%} (60.6\% $\to$ 67.9\%); and SealQA, a search-augmented QA benchmark with noisy retrieval, where it yields a \textbf{12.1\%} gain (26.6\% $\to$ 38.7\%). We also investigate the zero-shot transfer capabilties of skills evolved on one task to the other; in particular: skills evolved from SealQA transfers zero-shot to BrowseComp, improving accuracy by \textbf{5.3\%} without modification demonstrating that skill-level optimization produces transferable capabilities beyond the training task.

zh.title: EvoSkill：多代理系统中的自动化技能发现
zh.summary: 自进化框架通过故障分析和结构化改进自动发现可重用的代理技能，实现了7.3-12.1%的准确性改进，并展示了跨任务的零射击可转移性。

fr.title: EvoSkill : Découverte automatisée de compétences pour systèmes multi-agents
fr.summary: Cadre auto-évolutif découvrant automatiquement des compétences d'agents réutilisables grâce à l'analyse des défaillances et au perfectionnement structuré, réalisant des améliorations de précision de 7,3-12,1% avec une transférabilité zero-shot démontrée sur les tâches.

de.title: EvoSkill: Automatisierte Skillentdeckung für Multi-Agent-Systeme
de.summary: Selbstentwickelndes Framework, das automatisch wiederverwendbare Agent-Fähigkeiten durch Fehleranalyse und strukturierte Verfeinerung entdeckt, mit nachgewiesenen Genauigkeitsverbesserungen von 7,3-12,1% und demonstrierter Zero-Shot-Übertragbarkeit über Aufgaben hinweg.

es.title: EvoSkill: Descubrimiento automatizado de habilidades para sistemas multiagente
es.summary: Marco auto-evolucionante que descubre automáticamente habilidades de agentes reutilizables a través del análisis de fallos y refinamiento estructurado, logrando mejoras de precisión del 7,3-12,1% con transferibilidad zero-shot demostrada entre tareas.

title: Rethinking Code Similarity for Automated Algorithm Design with LLMs
summary: Novel metric measuring algorithmic similarity through problem-solving trajectories rather than code syntax, enabling better distinction of genuine algorithmic innovation and improving LLM-based algorithm design performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.02787v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rise of Large Language Model-based Automated Algorithm Design (LLM-AAD) has transformed algorithm development by autonomously generating code implementations of expert-level algorithms. Unlike traditional expert-driven algorithm development, in the LLM-AAD paradigm, the main design principle behind an algorithm is often implicitly embedded in the generated code. Therefore, assessing algorithmic similarity directly from code, distinguishing genuine algorithmic innovation from mere syntactic variation, becomes essential. While various code similarity metrics exist, they fail to capture algorithmic similarity, as they focus on surface-level syntax or output equivalence rather than the underlying algorithmic logic.
  We propose BehaveSim, a novel method to measure algorithmic similarity through the lens of problem-solving behavior as a sequence of intermediate solutions produced during execution, dubbed as problem-solving trajectories (PSTrajs). By quantifying the alignment between PSTrajs using dynamic time warping (DTW), BehaveSim distinguishes algorithms with divergent logic despite syntactic or output-level similarities. We demonstrate its utility in two key applications: (i) Enhancing LLM-AAD: Integrating BehaveSim into existing LLM-AAD frameworks (e.g., FunSearch, EoH) promotes behavioral diversity, significantly improving performance on three AAD tasks. (ii) Algorithm analysis: BehaveSim clusters generated algorithms by behavior, enabling systematic analysis of problem-solving strategies--a crucial tool for the growing ecosystem of AI-generated algorithms. Data and code of this work are open-sourced at https://github.com/RayZhhh/behavesim.

zh.title: 重新思考代码相似性以实现LLM自动化算法设计
zh.summary: 一种新颖的指标，通过问题解决轨迹而非代码语法来衡量算法相似性，能够更好地区分真正的算法创新，并提高基于LLM的算法设计性能。

fr.title: Repenser la similarité du code pour la conception automatique d'algorithmes avec les LLM
fr.summary: Une métrique nouvelle qui mesure la similarité algorithmique par les trajectoires de résolution de problèmes plutôt que par la syntaxe du code, permettant une meilleure distinction de l'innovation algorithmique véritable et améliorant les performances de conception d'algorithmes basés sur les LLM.

de.title: Umdenken der Code-Ähnlichkeit für automatisiertes Algorithmusdesign mit LLMs
de.summary: Eine neuartige Metrik, die algorithmische Ähnlichkeit durch Problemlösungsbahnen statt Code-Syntax misst, eine bessere Unterscheidung echter algorithmischer Innovation ermöglicht und die Leistung des LLM-basierten Algorithmusdesigns verbessert.

es.title: Replanteamiento de la similitud del código para el diseño automatizado de algoritmos con LLM
es.summary: Una métrica novedosa que mide la similitud algorítmica a través de trayectorias de resolución de problemas en lugar de sintaxis de código, permitiendo una mejor distinción de innovación algorítmica genuina y mejorando el rendimiento del diseño de algoritmos basado en LLM.

title: Agentified Assessment of Logical Reasoning Agents
summary: Agent-based evaluation framework enabling reproducible and auditable assessment of logical reasoning agents, achieving 86.7% accuracy on theorem proving through auto-formalization and SMT solver integration.
content:
arXiv:2603.02788v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present a framework for evaluating and benchmarking logical reasoning agents when assessment itself must be reproducible, auditable, and robust to execution failures. Building on agentified assessment, we use an assessor agent to issue tasks, enforce execution budgets, parse outputs, and record structured failure types, while the agent under test only needs to expose a standardized agent-to-agent interface. As a case study, we benchmark an auto-formalization agent for first-order logic (FOL) reasoning on a solver-verified and repaired split of FOLIO. The agent translates natural language premises and conclusions into executable Z3Py programs and employs satisfiability modulo theories (SMT) solving to determine logical entailment. On the cleaned FOLIO validation set, the auto-formalization agent achieves 86.70% accuracy under the assessor protocol, outperforming a chain-of-thought baseline (73.89%).

zh.title: Agent逻辑推理能力的认证评估
zh.summary: 基于Agent的评估框架，能够对逻辑推理Agent进行可复现和可审计的评估，通过自动形式化和SMT求解器集成在定理证明上达到86.7%的精度。

fr.title: Évaluation agentifiée de la capacité de raisonnement logique des agents
fr.summary: Cadre d'évaluation basé sur les agents permettant l'évaluation reproductible et vérifiable des agents de raisonnement logique, atteignant une précision de 86,7% sur la preuve de théorèmes grâce à l'auto-formalisation et l'intégration des solveurs SMT.

de.title: Agentifizierte Bewertung von logischen Argumentationsagenten
de.summary: Ein agentenbasiertes Bewertungsframework, das eine reproduzierbare und prüfbare Bewertung von Logik-Argumentationsagenten ermöglicht und durch Autoformalisierung und SMT-Solver-Integration 86,7% Genauigkeit beim Theorembeweis erreicht.

es.title: Evaluación agentificada del razonamiento lógico de los agentes
es.summary: Marco de evaluación basado en agentes que permite la evaluación reproducible y auditable del razonamiento lógico de los agentes, logrando una precisión del 86,7% en la demostración de teoremas a través de la formalización automática e integración del solucionador SMT.

title: Guideline-Grounded Evidence Accumulation for High-Stakes Agent Verification
summary: Agent verification framework using expert-curated clinical guidelines to evaluate high-stakes medical decisions, improving discrimination and calibration metrics on diagnostic tasks with practical clinician validation.
content:
arXiv:2603.02798v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As LLM-powered agents have been used for high-stakes decision-making, such as clinical diagnosis, it becomes critical to develop reliable verification of their decisions to facilitate trustworthy deployment. Yet, existing verifiers usually underperform owing to a lack of domain knowledge and limited calibration. To address this, we establish GLEAN, an agent verification framework with Guideline-grounded Evidence Accumulation that compiles expert-curated protocols into trajectory-informed, well-calibrated correctness signals. GLEAN evaluates the step-wise alignment with domain guidelines and aggregates multi-guideline ratings into surrogate features, which are accumulated along the trajectory and calibrated into correctness probabilities using Bayesian logistic regression. Moreover, the estimated uncertainty triggers active verification, which selectively collects additional evidence for uncertain cases via expanding guideline coverage and performing differential checks. We empirically validate GLEAN with agentic clinical diagnosis across three diseases from the MIMIC-IV dataset, surpassing the best baseline by 12% in AUROC and 50% in Brier score reduction, which confirms the effectiveness in both discrimination and calibration. In addition, the expert study with clinicians recognizes GLEAN's utility in practice.

zh.title: 基于指南的高风险Agent验证证据积累
zh.summary: Agent验证框架，使用专家策划的临床指南来评估高风险医疗决策，改进诊断任务的判别和校准指标，具有实际的临床医生验证。

fr.title: Accumulation de preuves fondée sur les lignes directrices pour la vérification des agents à enjeux élevés
fr.summary: Cadre de vérification des agents utilisant des directives cliniques sélectionnées par des experts pour évaluer les décisions médicales à enjeux élevés, améliorant les métriques de discrimination et de calibrage sur les tâches diagnostiques avec une validation pratique des cliniciens.

de.title: Richtliniengestützte Evidenzakkumulation zur Überprüfung von Hochrisikoagenten
de.summary: Agent-Verifizierungsrahmen, der von Experten kuratierte klinische Richtlinien nutzt, um hochriskante medizinische Entscheidungen zu bewerten und die Diskriminierungs- und Kalibrierungsmetriken bei Diagnoseaufgaben mit praktischer Kliniker-Validierung zu verbessern.

es.title: Acumulación de pruebas fundamentada en directrices para la verificación de agentes de alto riesgo
es.summary: Marco de verificación de agentes que utiliza directrices clínicas curadas por expertos para evaluar decisiones médicas de alto riesgo, mejorando las métricas de discriminación y calibración en tareas diagnósticas con validación clínica práctica.

title: LLM-based Argument Mining meets Argumentation and Description Logics: a Unified Framework for Reasoning about Debates
summary: Framework integrating LLM argument extraction with formal argumentation semantics and description logic querying for transparent, verifiable analysis of complex debates with explicit support and attack relations.
content:
arXiv:2603.02858v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve strong performance in analyzing and generating text, yet they struggle with explicit, transparent, and verifiable reasoning over complex texts such as those containing debates. In particular, they lack structured representations that capture how arguments support or attack each other and how their relative strengths determine overall acceptability. We encompass these limitations by proposing a framework that integrates learning-based argument mining with quantitative reasoning and ontology-based querying. Starting from a raw debate text, the framework extracts a fuzzy argumentative knowledge base, where arguments are explicitly represented as entities, linked by attack and support relations, and annotated with initial fuzzy strengths reflecting plausibility w.r.t. the debate's context. Quantitative argumentation semantics are then applied to compute final argument strengths by propagating the effects of supports and attacks. These results are then embedded into a fuzzy description logic setting, enabling expressive query answering through efficient rewriting techniques. The proposed approach provides a transparent, explainable, and formally grounded method for analyzing debates, overcoming purely statistical LLM-based analyses.

zh.title: 基于LLM的论证挖掘与论证和描述逻辑的结合：关于辩论推理的统一框架
zh.summary: 将LLM论证提取与形式论证语义和描述逻辑查询集成的框架，用于对复杂辩论进行透明、可验证的分析，具有明确的支持和攻击关系。

fr.title: L'extraction d'arguments basée sur LLM rencontre l'argumentation et les logiques de description : un cadre unifié pour le raisonnement sur les débats
fr.summary: Cadre intégrant l'extraction d'arguments LLM avec la sémantique de l'argumentation formelle et l'interrogation de la logique de description pour une analyse transparente et vérifiable des débats complexes avec des relations de soutien et d'attaque explicites.

de.title: LLM-basiertes Argument Mining trifft auf Argumentation und Beschreibungslogiken: Ein einheitliches Framework für die Argumentation über Debatten
de.summary: Framework, das LLM-Argumentextraktion mit formaler Argumentationssemantik und Beschreibungslogik-Abfragen integriert, um transparente, verifizierbare Analysen komplexer Debatten mit expliziten Unterstützungs- und Angriffsrelationen zu ermöglichen.

es.title: La minería de argumentos basada en LLM se encuentra con la argumentación y las lógicas de descripción: un marco unificado para razonar sobre debates
es.summary: Marco que integra la extracción de argumentos LLM con la semántica de argumentación formal y consultas de lógica de descripción para análisis transparentes y verificables de debates complejos con relaciones explícitas de apoyo y ataque.

title: Retrievit: In-context Retrieval Capabilities of Transformers, State Space Models, and Hybrid Architectures
summary: Comparative analysis showing hybrid Transformer-State Space Model architectures achieve comparable retrieval performance to Transformers with linear-time complexity, revealing fundamental differences in positional reasoning.
content:
arXiv:2603.02874v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Transformers excel at in-context retrieval but suffer from quadratic complexity with sequence length, while State Space Models (SSMs) offer efficient linear-time processing but have limited retrieval capabilities. We investigate whether hybrid architectures combining Transformers and SSMs can achieve the best of both worlds on two synthetic in-context retrieval tasks. The first task, n-gram retrieval, requires the model to identify and reproduce an n-gram that succeeds the query within the input sequence. The second task, position retrieval, presents the model with a single query token and requires it to perform a two-hop associative lookup: first locating the corresponding element in the sequence, and then outputting its positional index. Under controlled experimental conditions, we assess data efficiency, length generalization, robustness to out of domain training examples, and learned representations across Transformers, SSMs, and hybrid architectures. We find that hybrid models outperform SSMs and match or exceed Transformers in data efficiency and extrapolation for information-dense context retrieval. However, Transformers maintain superiority in position retrieval tasks. Through representation analysis, we discover that SSM-based models develop locality-aware embeddings where tokens representing adjacent positions become neighbors in embedding space, forming interpretable structures. This emergent property, absent in Transformers, explains both the strengths and limitations of SSMs and hybrids for different retrieval tasks. Our findings provide principled guidance for architecture selection based on task requirements and reveal fundamental differences in how Transformers and SSMs, and hybrid models learn positional associations.

zh.title: Retrievit：Transformer、状态空间模型和混合架构的上下文检索能力
zh.summary: 比较分析表明混合Transformer-状态空间模型架构实现了与Transformer相当的检索性能，同时具有线性时间复杂性，揭示了位置推理中的基本差异。

fr.title: Retrievit : Capacités de récupération en contexte des Transformers, des modèles d'espace d'état et des architectures hybrides
fr.summary: Une analyse comparative montrant que les architectures hybrides Transformer-State Space Model réalisent des performances de récupération comparables aux Transformers avec une complexité temporelle linéaire, révélant des différences fondamentales dans le raisonnement positionnel.

de.title: Retrievit: In-Kontext-Abruffähigkeiten von Transformers, State-Space-Modellen und hybriden Architekturen
de.summary: Vergleichende Analyse zeigt, dass Hybrid-Transformer-State-Space-Modell-Architekturen vergleichbare Abrufleistungen mit linearer Zeitkomplexität erreichen und grundlegende Unterschiede bei der Positionslogik offenbaren.

es.title: Retrievit: Capacidades de recuperación en contexto de Transformers, modelos de espacio de estados y arquitecturas híbridas
es.summary: Análisis comparativo que muestra que las arquitecturas híbridas de Transformer-State Space Model logran un rendimiento de recuperación comparable al de los Transformers con complejidad temporal lineal, revelando diferencias fundamentales en el razonamiento posicional.

title: SAE as a Crystal Ball: Interpretable Features Predict Cross-domain Transferability of LLMs without Training
summary: Metric using sparse autoencoders to forecast post-training transferability without fine-tuning, achieving high predictive accuracy and enabling informed selection of post-training strategies for cross-domain performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.02908v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In recent years, pre-trained large language models have achieved remarkable success across diverse tasks. Besides the pivotal role of self-supervised pre-training, their effectiveness in downstream applications also depends critically on the post-training process, which adapts models to task-specific data and objectives. However, this process inevitably introduces model shifts that can influence performance in different domains, and how such shifts transfer remains poorly understood. To open up the black box, we propose the SAE-based Transferability Score (STS), a new metric that leverages sparse autoencoders (SAEs) to forecast post-training transferability. Taking supervised fine-tuning as an example, STS identifies shifted dimensions in SAE representations and calculates their correlations with downstream domains, enabling reliable estimation of transferability \textit{before} fine-tuning. Extensive experiments across multiple models and domains show that STS accurately predicts the transferability of supervised fine-tuning, achieving Pearson correlation coefficients above 0.7 with actual performance changes. Beyond this, we take an initial step toward extending STS to reinforcement learning. We believe that STS can serve as an {\color{black} interpretable} tool for guiding post-training strategies in LLMs. Code is available at https://github.com/PKU-ML/STS.

zh.title: SAE作为水晶球：可解释特征预测LLM的跨域可迁移性而无需训练
zh.summary: 使用稀疏自编码器预测训练后可迁移性而无需微调的度量，实现高预测准确性，并支持跨域性能的训练后策略的知情选择。

fr.title: SAE comme une boule de cristal : les fonctionnalités interprétables prédisent la transférabilité inter-domaines des LLM sans entraînement
fr.summary: Métrique utilisant des auto-encodeurs clairsemés pour prévoir la transférabilité post-entraînement sans fine-tuning, réalisant une haute précision prédictive et permettant une sélection éclairée des stratégies post-entraînement pour la performance inter-domaines.

de.title: SAE als Kristallkugel: Interpretierbare Merkmale prognostizieren die domänenübergreifende Übertragbarkeit von LLMs ohne Training
de.summary: Metrik unter Verwendung spärlicher Autoencodierer zur Vorhersage der Übertragbarkeit nach dem Training ohne Fine-Tuning, erreichende hohe Vorhersagegenauigkeit und ermöglichende informierte Auswahl von Strategien nach dem Training für domänenübergreifende Leistung.

es.title: SAE como bola de cristal: características interpretables predicen la transferibilidad entre dominios de LLM sin entrenamiento
es.summary: Métrica que utiliza codificadores automáticos dispersos para pronosticar la transferibilidad posterior al entrenamiento sin ajuste fino, logrando alta precisión predictiva y permitiendo la selección informada de estrategias posteriores al entrenamiento para el desempeño entre dominios.
